content: Toluene on reaction with reagent $A$ gives $X$. This $(X)$ forms 2, 4 dinitrophenylhydrazone and reduces ammoniacal silver nitrate solution. Reaction of toluene with another reagent $B$ forms $Y$, which dissolves in $\mathrm{NaHCO}_3$ with evolution of $\mathrm{CO}_2$. What are $A$ and $B$ respectively?
explanation:
The complete reaction sequence is as follows

The benzaldehyde ( $X$ ) formed can form a 2,4-dinitrophenylhydrazone derivative and reduces ammoniacal silver nitrate solution.

Now,

Here, toluene reacts with $\mathrm{KMnO}_4-\mathrm{KOH} / \Delta, \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{O}^{+}$to form benzoic acid $(Y)$. The benzoic acid dissolves in $\mathrm{NaHCO}_3$, with the evolution of $\mathrm{CO}_2$.

Thus, $A$ and $B$ respectively are $\mathrm{CrO}_3+\left(\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CO}\right)_2 \mathrm{O}, \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{O}^{+}$and $\mathrm{KMnO}_4-\mathrm{KOH} / \Delta, \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{O}^{+}$.

content: The products $C$ and $D$ are

$$ \text { Toluene } \xrightarrow[\text { (ii) } \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{O}^{+}]{\text {(i) } \mathrm{CrO}_2 \mathrm{Cl}_2 / \mathrm{CS}_2} X \xrightarrow{\text { Conc. } \mathrm{NaOH}} Y+Z $$

The correct statements about $Y$ and $Z$ are

A. $Y$ is a secondary alcohol.

B. $Y$ is the reduction product of $X$.

C. $Z$ on heating with sodalime gives benzene.

D. $Y$ does not give $\mathrm{H}_2$ gas with Na metal.
explanation:
$$ \text { The complete reaction sequence is as follows. } $$

Thus, statement given in $B$ and $C$ are correct. i.e., $Y$ is the reduction product of $X$ and $Z$ on heating with sodalime gives benzene.

content:
Assertion (A) : Aldehydes are more reactive than ketones towards nucleophilic addition reactions

Reason (R) : In aldehydes, carbonyl carbon is less electrophilic compared to ketones.

The correct answer is

options.content: (A) and (R) are correct (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
(A) and (R) are correct, but $(R)$ is not the correct explanation of (A).
(A) is correct but $(R)$ is not correct.
(A) is not correct but (R) is correct.

explanation:
A is correct, but R is not correct. The correct form of R is: Ketones are less electrophilic than aldehyde.

The positive inductive effect of second alkyl radical reinforces that of the firsy one decreasing still further the partial positive charge on carbonyl carbon atom. This reduce the attraction of aton for nucleophilic reagents. Hence, ketones are less electrophilic.

content: The product( $s$ ) formed when $m$-chlorobenzaldehyde is heated with concentrated NaOH is/are
explanation: m-chlorobenzaldehyde on reaction with conc. NaOH at room temperature gives potassium $m$-chlorobenzoate and m-chlorobenzyl alcohol.

content:
Statement I Aldehyde on reaction with HCN gives cyanohydrin.

Statement II Cyanohydrin is a compound which consists of hydroxy and cyano groups on the same carbon.

Choose the correct answer from the following with reference to above statements.

options.content: Both statements I and II are false.
Both statements I and II are true.
Statement I is true and Statement II is false.
Statement I is false and Statement II is true.

explanation:
Cyanohydrin reaction occurs when an aldehyde gets treated with a HCN forming a cyanohydrin product.

Cyanohydrin is a compound which consist of hydroxy and cyano group on same carbon atom.

Hence, Statement I and Statement II are true.

content: What is the major product ' $C$ ' in the following sequence of reactions?

$A$ undergoes hydration in presence of $\mathrm{Hg}^{2+} / \mathrm{H}^{+}$to give unsaturated alcohol ${ }^{\prime} B .^{\prime} B$ ' undergoes tautomerism to form ' $C$ as major product.

content: Which of the following represents Gatterman-Koch reaction?
explanation: In Gattermann-Koch reaction, benzene is treated with carbon monoxide ( CO ) in acidic medium in presence of anhy. $\mathrm{AlCl}_3$ to give benzaldehyde.

content: In the reaction sequence, conversion of $B$ to $C$ is known as
explanation: Sodium benzoate is heated with mixture of sodium hydroxide ( NaOH ) and CaO to give benzene (A). A undergoes Friedel-Crafts alkylation reaction to give toluene (B). B undergoes oxidation with chromyl chloride ( $\mathrm{CrO}_2 \mathrm{Cl}_2$ ) in $\mathrm{CS}_2$ to give benzaldehyde $(\mathrm{C})$. This conversion of (B) to $(\mathrm{C})$ is known as Etard's reaction.

explanation:
The compound $(Z)$ formed undergoes Wolff-Kishner reduction and it also with yellow precipitate with $\mathrm{I}_2$ and NaOH solution.

Thus, statements given in I and III correct.

content: A primary alcohol was reacted with pyridinium chlorochromate (PCC), which resulted in a product $P$. The product $P$ on treatment with ammonical silver nitrate solution produces
explanation: Firstly, primary alcohol is converted to aldehyde $(P)$ using an oxidising agent PCC. The product $P$ then react with alkaline solution of ammonium silver nitrate to form carboxylate anion and silver mirror.

content: Benzene on reaction with acetyl chloride in the presence of anhydrous $\mathrm{AlCl}_3$ gave the product $P$. The product $P$ on reaction with methylmagnesium bromide followed by treatment with water furnished the product $Q$. The molecular formula of $Q$ is
explanation:
Benzene reacts with acetyl chloride in presence of anhydrous $\mathrm{AlCl}_3$ to give Friedel crafts acylation reaction. This reaction follows electrophilic substitution reaction. The product, $P$ formed is acetophenone.

$P$ on reacting with methyl magnesium bromide ( MeMgBr ) gives alkoxide product which upon hydrolysis gives alcohol product.

$$ \text { Molecular formula of } Q \text { is } \mathrm{C}_9 \mathrm{H}_{12} \mathrm{O} \text {. } $$

content:
$$ \text { Match the following. } $$

List-I List-ii
(A) Acid chloride to aldehyde (I) $$
\text { DIBAL-H }
$$
(B) Benzene to benzaldehyde (II) $$
\mathrm{CO}, \mathrm{HCl} \text {, anhyd. } \mathrm{AlCl}_3
$$
(C) Acetylene to aldehyde (III) $$
\mathrm{HgSO}_4, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4
$$
(D) Ester to aldehyde (IV) $$
\mathrm{H}_2, \mathrm{Pd}-\mathrm{BaSO}_4
$$

options.content:
A B C D
IV II I III

A B C D
I II III IV

A B C D
III II I IV

A B C D
IV II III I

explanation:
(A) Acid chlorides on treatment with $\mathrm{H}_2$, $\mathrm{Pd}-\mathrm{BaSO}_4$ form aldehyde. This reaction is called "Rosenmund reaction".

(B) Benzene can be converted to benzaldehyde by reacting it with $\mathrm{CO}, \mathrm{HCl}$, anhydrous $\mathrm{AlCl}_3$. This reaction is known as "Gatterman-Koch reaction".

(C) Alkene (acetylene) can be converted to aldehyde (acetaldehyde) by reacting it with $\mathrm{HgSO}_4$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$. This reaction is called "oxymercuration-demercuration reaction".

$$ \text { (D) DIBAL-H reagent is used to convert esters into aldehydes. } $$

content: 1. The major product formed in the following reaction sequence is
explanation:
(i) Reaction of HBr with propene in the presence of peroxide gives $n$-propyl bromide. This addition reaction is an example of an anti-Markownikoff's addition reaction.

$$ \begin{aligned} & R-\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{R} \xrightarrow{\Delta} 2 \mathrm{R}-\mathrm{O}^* \\ & 2 \mathrm{R}-\mathrm{O}^*+\mathrm{H}-\mathrm{Br} \longrightarrow \mathrm{R}-\mathrm{OH}+\mathrm{Br}^* \end{aligned} $$

(ii) Reaction of $n$-propyl bromide with Mg gives propyl magnesium bromide (Grignard reagent).

(iii) Reaction of propyl magnesium bromide with propanal gives nucleophilic additon reaction.

(iv) Reaction of hexan-3-ol with oxidising agent $\mathrm{CrO}_3$.

content:
Which of the following reactions will give benzophenone as major product?

(A) Benzoyl chloride + benzene $+\mathrm{AlCl}_3$ (anhyd).

(B) Benzoyl chloride + phenylmagnesium bromide

     (excess)

(C) Benzoyl chloride + diphenyl cadmium
explanation:
(A) Reaction of benzoyl chloride with benzene and aluminium chloride gives benzophenone.

(B) Reaction of benzoyl chloride with phenyl magnesium bromide (excess).

(C) Reaction of benzoyl chloride with diphenyl cadmium gives benzophenone.
